info.title: API Documentation
info.description:
## Overview

This API allows you to manage your account and servers programmatically. Account management features are available to all authenticated users, while server management operations are restricted to server owners. Only users who own a server can perform actions such as creating, or managing through the API.

### Authentication

The API uses **Bearer Token authorization** for all authenticated requests.

To access protected endpoints, include your API token in the `Authorization` header of each request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your_api_token>
```

If the token is missing, invalid, or expired, the API will respond with:
```
HTTP 401 Unauthorized
```

Tokens are issued through your account and must be kept secure.

### Data Format

The API uses **JSON** for all requests and responses. All requests should be sent with the following headers:

```
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json
```

### Rate Limiting

The API is rate limited to prevent excessive requests and ensure fair usage. Each response includes rate limit headers that indicate your current usage:
| Header | Description |
| ------- | ------------ |
| **X-RateLimit-Limit** | The total number of requests allowed per minute. |
| **X-RateLimit-Remaining** | The number of requests remaining in the current time window. |

When the limit is reached, the API will respond with the following status until the limit resets:

```
HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
```

### Service Availability

If the API is temporarily unavailable or undergoing maintenance, it will return the following status:

```
HTTP 503 Service Unavailable
```

This status indicates a temporary outage and the request should be retried later once the service is restored.

post.description: Use this endpoint to build (or rebuild) the specified server. Before running the build method, you can use the other related endpoints to retrieve the required values, such as Operating System Templates and Swap Options to include in your request.

get.description: Retrieves the list of available operating system templates for the specified server. The template IDs provided in the response can be used when building a server.

data.name: Debian 13 (Trixie) Minimal
data.description: Minimal installation with limited packages on an ext4 filesystem. New packages are easily installed using Advanced Package Tool (APT), the main command-line package manager for Debian.

data.name: Debian 12 (Bookworm) ext4
data.description: Minimal installation with limited packages on an ext4 filesystem. New packages are easily installed using Advanced Package Tool (APT), the main command-line package manager for Debian.

data.name: CentOS 7 Minimal
data.description: Minimal installation with limited packages. New packages are easily installed using Yum, the main command-line package manager for CentOS.

data.name: CentOS Stream 9 Minimal
data.description: Base installation with limited packages. New packages are easily installed using DNF (yum), the main command-line package manager for CentOS.

data.name: Rocky Linux 10
data.description: Minimal installation with limited packages. New packages are easily installed using DNF (yum), the main command-line package manager for Rocky Linux.

data.name: AlmaLinux 10 Latest
data.description: Latest version with base packages. New packages are easily installed using DNF (yum), the main command-line package manager for AlmaLinux.

data.name: AlmaLinux 10 Latest (x86-64-v2)
data.description: Latest x86-64-v2 (support for older hardware) version with base packages. New packages are easily installed using DNF (yum), the main command-line package manager for AlmaLinux.

data.name: AlmaLinux Kitten 10
data.description: AlmaLinux OS Kitten is the direct upstream for AlmaLinux OS 10, and is the primary point for the AlmaLinux community to engage and influence the future of AlmaLinux OS. Fixes and features land here first and trickle down into AlmaLinux OS as appropriate. It is the integration and collaboration point for AlmaLinux to its upstreams as well, such as CentOS Stream and Fedora.

data.description: AlmaLinux OS Kitten is the direct upstream for AlmaLinux OS 10, and is the primary point for the AlmaLinux community to engage and influence the future of AlmaLinux OS. Fixes and features land here first and trickle down into AlmaLinux OS as appropriate. It is the integration and collaboration point for AlmaLinux to its upstreams as well, such as CentOS Stream and Fedora.

data.name: openSUSE Leap 15 Minimal
data.description:
openSUSE is a project that serves to promote the use of free and open-source software.

Minimal installation with limited packages. New packages are easily installed using Zypper, the main command-line package manager for openSUSE.

data.name: Oracle Linux 9 Minimal
data.description:
Oracle Linux operating system is engineered for open cloud infrastructure. It delivers leading performance, scalability, reliability and security for enterprise SaaS and PaaS workloads as well as traditional enterprise applications.

Minimal installation with limited packages. New packages are easily installed using DNF (yum), the main command-line package manager for Oracle Linux.
